message:
bench(fcp-host): add cold_start criterion benchmark

Adds a criterion-driven cold-start benchmark for the fcp-host process startup
path, covering the boot-to-readiness latency over a TCP-loopback connector
inventory.

Files:

- crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml
  Adds criterion as a workspace dev-dep and registers the new bench target:
    [[bench]]
    name = "cold_start"
    harness = false  # criterion drives its own harness

- crates/fcp-host/benches/cold_start.rs (new, 5.1 KiB)
  The benchmark itself:
  - Locates the compiled fcp-host and connector binaries via env-var override
    (FCP_HOST_BIN / FCP_BENCH_CONNECTOR_BIN) or by walking up from the
    benchmark exe path to target/<profile>/.
  - Picks a free loopback port via ephemeral TcpListener bind.
  - Builds a one-shot connector inventory JSON for fcp.bench.cold-start
    utility 1.0.0 and feeds it into the host on stdin.
  - Times boot-to-readiness with a 10-second readiness timeout, returning
    a Duration that criterion samples across iterations.

- Cargo.lock
  Materializes criterion as a transitive dep of fcp-host. Workspace already
  carries criterion in [workspace.dependencies], so no upstream resolution
  changes — only the lockfile entry for the fcp-host package gets the new
  edge.

Pure dev-only addition: no production source paths in fcp-host were touched,
the bench is harness=false (criterion-driven), and criterion is gated under
[dev-dependencies] so it never reaches release builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,3 +60,8 @@ fcp-testkit = { path = "../fcp-testkit" }
 reqwest.workspace = true
 tempfile = "3"
 insta.workspace = true
+criterion.workspace = true
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "cold_start"
+harness = false
